questions.domain: 領域 3：雲端技術與服務 (Cloud Technology and Services)

question_text.zh: 一家公司使用 Amazon RDS 作为产品数据库。该公司希望确保数据库具有高可用性。 Amazon RDS 的哪些功能可以满足此要求？
question_text.en: A company uses Amazon RDS for a product database. The company wants to ensure the database is highly available. Which feature of Amazon RDS will meet this requirement?

A.zh: 读取副本
A.en: Read replicas

B.zh: 蓝/绿部署
B.en: Blue/green deployment

C.zh: 多可用区部署
C.en: Multi-AZ deployment

D.zh: 预留实例
D.en: Reserved Instances

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：只读副本用于分担数据库的读取负载，但它不提供高可用性，因为它无法自动接管主数据库的写入操作。如果主数据库发生故障，只读副本无法取代主数据库。因此不符合题目要求。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: A read replica is used to share the read load of a database, but it does not provide high availability because it cannot automatically take over the write operations of the primary database. If the primary database fails, the read replica cannot replace the primary database. Therefore this option is wrong.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：蓝图部署是一种应用程序部署策略，用于减少停机时间和风险，但与数据库的高可用性无关。因此不符合题目要求。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Blueprint deployment is an application deployment strategy that is used to reduce downtime and risk, but it has nothing to do with high availability of the database. Therefore this option is wrong.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：多可用区是 Amazon RDS 提供的一项高可用性功能。它在多个可用区中创建备用实例，并在主实例发生故障时自动切换到备用实例，从而确保数据库的高可用性。因此该选项符合题目要求。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: Multi-AZ is a high-availability feature provided by Amazon RDS. It creates a standby instance in multiple availability zones and automatically switches to the standby instance when the primary instance fails, thus ensuring high availability of the database. Therefore this option is.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：预留实例 是用于节省成本的定价选项，与高可用性无关。因此不符合题目要求。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: are a pricing option used to save costs and have nothing to do with high availability. Therefore this option is wrong.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：多可用区是 Amazon RDS 提供的一项高可用性功能。它在多个可用区中创建备用实例，并在主实例发生故障时自动切换到备用实例，从而确保数据库的高可用性。因此该选项符合题目要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Multi-AZ is a high-availability feature provided by Amazon RDS. It creates a standby instance in multiple availability zones and automatically switches to the standby instance when the primary instance fails, thus ensuring high availability of the database. Therefore this option is.

questions.domain: 領域 3：雲端技術與服務 (Cloud Technology and Services)

question_text.zh: 哪一种 AWS 服务提供与容器一起使用的无服务器计算？
question_text.en: Which AWS service provides serverless compute for use with containers?

A.zh: Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS)
A.en: Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS)

B.zh: AWS Fargate
B.en: AWS Fargate

C.zh: AWS Elastic Beanstalk
C.en: AWS Elastic Beanstalk

D.zh: Amazon SageMaker
D.en: Amazon SageMaker

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon SQS 是一种消息队列服务，旨在解耦和扩展分布式系统。它与容器没有直接关系，也不提供计算能力，因此不符合题目要求。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon SQS is a message queuing service designed to decouple and scale distributed systems. It is not directly related to containers and does not provide computing power, so this option is wrong.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS Fargate 是一种无服务器计算引擎，旨在运行容器而无需管理底层服务器或集群。该选项符合题目要求。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: is a serverless compute engine designed to run containers without the need to manage underlying servers or clusters. This option is.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Elastic Beanstalk 是一个用于部署和管理应用程序的平台即服务 (PaaS)，但它不是专用的无服务器容器计算服务，因此不符合题目要求。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: is a platform as a service (PaaS) for deploying and managing applications, but it is not a dedicated serverless container computing service, so this option is.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon SageMaker 是一种用于构建、训练和部署模型的机器学习服务，与容器无服务器计算没有直接关系，因此不符合题目要求。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: is a machine learning service for building, training, and deploying models and is not directly related to container serverless computing, so this option is.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS Fargate 是一种无服务器计算引擎，旨在运行容器而无需管理底层服务器或集群。该选项符合题目要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: is a serverless compute engine designed to run containers without the need to manage underlying servers or clusters. This option is.

questions.domain: 領域 4：計費、定價與支援 (Billing, Pricing, and Support)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在为不同的业务团队使用多个 AWS 账户。财务团队希望收到一张针对公司所有账户的账单。财务团队应使用哪种 AWS 服务或工具来满足此要求？
question_text.en: A company is using multiple AWS accounts for different business teams. The finance team wants to receive one bill for all of the company's accounts. Which AWS service or tool should the finance team use to meet this requirement?

A.zh: AWS Organizations
A.en: AWS Organizations

B.zh: AWS Trusted Advisor
B.en: AWS Trusted Advisor

C.zh: 成本管理器
C.en: Cost Explorer

D.zh: AWS Budgets
D.en: AWS Budgets

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS Organizations 允许公司集中管理多个 AWS 账户并启用合并账单，以满足希望收到一张账单的财务团队的需求。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: AWS Organizations allows companies to centrally manage multiple AWS accounts and enable Consolidated Billing to meet the needs of financial teams who want to receive one bill.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Trusted Advisor 是一个用于检查账户安全性、成本效率、性能等的优化工具，但它不提供单一计费功能。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: AWS Trusted Advisor is an optimization tool used to check account security, cost efficiency, performance, etc., but it does not provide single billing functionality.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Cost Explorer 是用于查看和分析成本和使用情况的分析工具，但它不负责产生单一账单。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: Cost Explorer is an analysis tool used to view and analyze costs and usage, but it is not responsible for generating a single bill.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Budgets 用于设置预算和控制成本，但不提供单一计费功能。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: AWS Budgets is used to set up budget and control costs, but it does not provide single billing functionality.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：A (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS Organizations 允许公司集中管理多个 AWS 账户并启用合并账单，以满足希望收到一张账单的财务团队的需求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This option is described as follows: AWS Organizations allows companies to centrally manage multiple AWS accounts and enable Consolidated Billing to meet the needs of financial teams who want to receive one bill.

questions.domain: 領域 2：安全與合規 (Security and Compliance)

question_text.zh: 公司需要一个防火墙来控制进出单一 Amazon EC2 实例的网络连接。此防火墙不会控制同一子网中其他实例的网络连接。公司可以使用哪些 AWS 服务或功能来满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company needs a firewall that will control network connections to and from a single Amazon EC2 instance. This firewall will not control network connections to and from other instances that are in the same subnet. Which AWS service or feature can the company use to meet these requirements?

A.zh: 网络 ACL
A.en: Network ACL

B.zh: AWS WAF
B.en: AWS WAF

C.zh: 路由表
C.en: Route table

D.zh: 安全群组
D.en: Security group

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：网络ACL控制整个子网的流量，无法在单一EC2实例上精确控制。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: Network ACL controls the flow of the entire subnet and cannot be precisely controlled on a single EC2 instance.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS WAF 主要用于保护 Web 应用程序免受常见网络攻击（例如 SQL 注入和跨站点脚本攻击），而不是控制流向 EC2 实例的网络流量。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: AWS WAF is primarily used to protect web applications from common network attacks (such as SQL injection and cross-site scripting attacks), rather than controlling network traffic to EC2 instances.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：路由表用于定义子网𪥿VPC的路由规则并控制流量的方向，但不能直接控制单一EC2实例的网络连接。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: Route table is used to define the routing rules of the subnet 𪥿VPC and control the direction of traffic, but it cannot directly control the network connection of a single EC2 instance.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因：安全群组是实例层级虚拟防火墙，可为单一 EC2 实例的入站和出站流量提供精确控制。
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: Security groups are instance-level virtual firewalls that provide precise control of inbound and outbound traffic for a single EC2 instance.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：D (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：安全群组是实例层级虚拟防火墙，可为单一 EC2 实例的入站和出站流量提供精确控制。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This option is described as follows: Security groups are instance-level virtual firewalls that provide precise control of inbound and outbound traffic for a single EC2 instance.

questions.domain: 領域 3：雲端技術與服務 (Cloud Technology and Services)

question_text.zh: 一家公司计划使用 Amazon EC2 实例作为 Web 服务器。来自世界各地的客户将使用网页服务器。大多数客户只会在一天中的特定时间使用网页服务器。公司应如何部署 EC2 实例以实现最低的运维成本？
question_text.en: A company is planning to use the Amazon EC2 instances as web servers. Customers from around the world will use the web servers. Most customers will use the web servers only during certain hours of the day. How should the company deploy the EC2 instances to achieve the LOWEST operational cost?

A.zh: 在多个可用区
A.en: In multiple Availability Zones

B.zh: 在 Auto Scaling 组中
B.en: In an Auto Scaling group

C.zh: 在置放群组中
C.en: In a placement group

D.zh: 在私有子网中
D.en: In private subnets

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：跨多个可用区部署可以提高可用性，但本身不会根据使用时段减少实例数量，不能实现最低运营成本。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Deploying EC2 instances in multiple availability zones can improve high availability, but it cannot directly reduce operating costs. The cup option does not exactly match the "lowest operating cost" required by the question. The cup option is wrong.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：Auto Scaling 组可根据流量自动调整 EC2 实例数量，并在非高峰时段减少实例，从而满足需求并降低运营成本。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: Auto Scaling groups can automatically adjust the number of EC2 instances based on traffic needs to avoid resource consumption. When customers only use services during scheduled hours, the Auto Scaling group can reduce the number of instances during off-peak hours, thereby reducing operating costs. The cup options meet the requirements of the question, so the cup is.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：置放群组用于优化实例放置和网络性能，不能根据使用时段自动减少实例数量。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Placement group is used to optimize network performance and communication between instances, but has nothing to do with reducing operating costs. The cup option does not match the "minimum operating cost" required by the question. The cup option is wrong.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：私有子网可增强网络隔离，但不能根据使用时段自动减少实例数量。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Deploying EC2 instances in private subnets can improve security, but has nothing to do with reducing operating costs. The cup option does not match the "minimum operating cost" required by the question. The cup option is wrong.

answer_text.zh: B. 在 Auto Scaling 组中
answer_text.en: B. In an Auto Scaling group

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：Auto Scaling 组可根据流量自动调整 EC2 实例数量，并在非高峰时段减少实例，从而满足需求并降低运营成本。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Auto Scaling groups can automatically adjust the number of EC2 instances based on traffic needs to avoid resource consumption. When customers only use services during scheduled hours, the Auto Scaling group can reduce the number of instances during off-peak hours, thereby reducing operating costs. The cup options meet the requirements of the question, so the cup is.

questions.domain: 領域 4：計費、定價與支援 (Billing, Pricing, and Support)

question_text.zh: 无论用户的 AWS 支持计划为何，AWS 的哪一项权益始终免费？
question_text.en: Which benefit is always free of charge with AWS, regardless of a user’s AWS Support plan?

A.zh: AWS 开发人员支持
A.en: AWS Developer Support

B.zh: AWS 开发者论坛
B.en: AWS Developer Forums

C.zh: 进程化个案管理
C.en: Programmatic case management

D.zh: AWS 技术客户经理 (TAM)
D.en: AWS technical account manager (TAM)

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Developer Support 属于付费支持计划的一部分，用户需要订阅该方案才能享受相关服务。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: AWS Developer Support is part of the paid support plan, and users need to subscribe to the plan to enjoy related services.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS 开发人员论坛是一个免费的社群支持平台，对所有 AWS 用户免费，不需要订阅任何支持方案。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: AWS Developer Forums is a free community support platform that is free to all AWS users and does not require a subscription to any support plan.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：进程化个案管理是与付费支持计划相关的一项功能，通常需要订阅更高层级的支持计划。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: Programmatic case management is a feature associated with paid support plans and typically requires a subscription to a higher level support plan.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：技术客户经理 (TAM) 是企业支持计划的一部分，仅适用于订阅企业支持计划的用户。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Technical Account Manager (TAM) is part of the Enterprise Support Plan and is only available to users who subscribe to the Enterprise Support Plan.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS 开发人员论坛是一个免费的社群支持平台，对所有 AWS 用户免费，不需要订阅任何支持方案。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This option is described as follows: AWS Developer Forums is a free community support platform that is free to all AWS users and does not require a subscription to any support plan.

questions.domain: 領域 4：計費、定價與支援 (Billing, Pricing, and Support)

question_text.zh: 一家公司使用 Amazon EC2 实例来运行其应用程序。该应用程序需要可用并连续运行三年或更长时间。公司应该以 EC2 定价折扣购买哪种类型的 EC2 实例？
question_text.en: A company uses Amazon EC2 instances to run its application. The application needs to be available and running continuously for three or more years. What type of EC2 instance should the company purchase for a discount on the EC2 pricing?

A.zh: 预留实例
A.en: Reserved Instances

B.zh: 竞价型实例
B.en: Spot Instances

C.zh: 按需型实例
C.en: On-Demand Instances

D.zh: EC2 舰队
D.en: EC2 Fleet

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：预留实例适合长期使用场景。用户可通过预付费获得大幅折扣，适合需要连续运行三年及以上的应用程序。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: Reserved Instances are suitable for long-term use scenarios. Users can obtain significant discounts through prepayment, which is suitable for applications that need to run continuously for three years or more.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：竞价型实例价格低廉，但它们是基于AWS的闲置容量提供的，并且可能随时中断，因此不适合需要持续运行的应用程序。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: Spot Instances are low-priced, but they are provided based on AWS's idle capacity and may be interrupted at any time, making them unsuitable for applications that require continuous operation.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：按需型实例按量付费，弹性高，但价格较高，不适合长期应用，没有折扣。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: On-Demand Instances are pay-as-you-go and have high flexibility, but the price is high and not suitable for long-term applications because there are no discounts.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：EC2 队列是一种组合选项，可以混合不同类型的实例，但它不是定价模型，不能直接提供长期折扣。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: EC2 Fleet is a portfolio option that can mix different types of instances, but it is not a pricing model and cannot directly provide long-term discounts.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：A (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：预留实例适合长期使用场景。用户可通过预付费获得大幅折扣，适合需要连续运行三年及以上的应用程序。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This option is described as follows: Reserved Instances are suitable for long-term use scenarios. Users can obtain significant discounts through prepayment, which is suitable for applications that need to run continuously for three years or more.

questions.domain: 領域 3：雲端技術與服務 (Cloud Technology and Services)

question_text.zh: 公司需要对最近的 AWS 账户活动进行审核。审计将调查谁发起了事件以及运行了哪些操作。公司应该使用哪种 AWS 服务来满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company needs to perform an audit of recent AWS account activity. The audit will investigate who initiated an event and what actions were performed. Which AWS service should the company use to meet these requirements?

A.zh: AWS Config
A.en: AWS Config

B.zh: Amazon Rekognition
B.en: Amazon Rekognition

C.zh: AWS CloudTrail
C.en: AWS CloudTrail

D.zh: Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS)
D.en: Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS)

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Config 主要用于资源配置的记录和合规性检查。虽然它可以记录资源的状态变化，但它并不专注于追踪用户活动或事件发起者，因此不适合审计用户活动。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: is mainly used for recording and compliance checking of resource configuration. Although it can record status changes of resources, it does not focus on tracking user activities or event initiators, so it is not suitable for auditing user activities.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon Rekognition 是用于人脸识别、对象检测等的图像和视频分析服务，与审核用户活动无关，因此不符合题目要求。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: is an image and video analysis service used for facial recognition, object detection, etc., and has nothing to do with auditing user activity, so this option is.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS CloudTrail 是一项旨在记录 AWS 账户活动的服务，包括谁发起了事件以及运行了哪些操作。它是审计用户活动的正确选择。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: is a service designed to record AWS account activity, including who initiated the event and what actions were performed. It is the right choice for auditing user activities.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon SNS 是用于发送通知或消息的消息服务。它不记录用户活动，因此不适合用于审核目的。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon SNS is a messaging service used to send notifications or messages. It does not log user activity and is therefore not suitable for auditing purposes.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS CloudTrail 是一项旨在记录 AWS 账户活动的服务，包括谁发起了事件以及运行了哪些操作。它是审计用户活动的正确选择。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: is a service designed to record AWS account activity, including who initiated the event and what actions were performed. It is the right choice for auditing user activities.

questions.domain: 領域 1：雲端概念 (Cloud Concepts)

question_text.zh: AWS Well-Architected Framework的可靠支柱包含哪些设计原则？ （选两个。）
question_text.en: Which design principles are included in the reliability pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework? (Choose two.)

A.zh: 自动从故障中恢复。
A.en: Automatically recover from failure.

B.zh: 授予每个人增加 AWS 服务配额的权限。
B.en: Grant everyone access to increase AWS service quotas.

C.zh: 停止猜测容量。
C.en: Stop guessing capacity.

D.zh: 设计应用程序以在单一可用区中运行。
D.en: Design applications to run in a single Availability Zone.

E.zh: 计划首先在辅助 AWS 区域增加 AWS 服务配额。
E.en: Plan to increase AWS service quotas first in a secondary AWS Region.

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：可靠性支柱强调系统自动从故障中恢复的能力，例如通过自动监控和故障处理机制。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: The reliability pillar emphasizes the system's ability to automatically recover from failures, such as through automated monitoring and fault-handling mechanisms.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：授予每个人访问权限与安全性相关，而不是可靠性支柱的设计原则。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Granting access to everyone is related to security, not a design principle of the reliability pillar.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：可靠性支柱强调使用自动扩展和弹性设计，以避免容量配置不足或过度。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: The reliability pillar emphasizes the use of autoscaling and elastic design to avoid under- or over-provisioning of capacity.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：可靠性支柱建议跨多个可用区设计应用程序以提高容错能力，而不是将操作限制在单一可用区。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The reliability pillar recommends designing applications across multiple Availability Zones to increase fault tolerance, rather than restricting operations to a single Availability Zone.

E.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：可靠性支柱不建议增加次要区域的服务配额，这不符合容量规划的核心原则。
E.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The reliability pillar does not recommend increasing service quotas in secondary regions, which is inconsistent with the core principles of capacity planning.

answer_text.zh: A. 自动从故障中恢复。；C. 停止猜测容量。
answer_text.en: A. Automatically recover from failure.; C. Stop guessing capacity.

discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: AC (67%) AE (33%). Popular discussion and key point summary: The reliability pillar emphasizes the system's ability to automatically recover from failures, such as through automated monitoring and fault-handling mechanisms.; The reliability pillar emphasizes the use of autoscaling and elastic design to avoid under- or over-provisioning of capacity.

questions.domain: 領域 3：雲端技術與服務 (Cloud Technology and Services)

question_text.zh: 某公司需要使用AWS技术部署静态网站。哪种解决方案能够以最少的运维开销满足此要求？
question_text.en: A company needs to use AWS technology to deploy a static website. Which solution meets this requirement with the LEAST amount of operational overhead?

A.zh: 在 Amazon EC2 上部署网站。
A.en: Deploy the website on Amazon EC2.

B.zh: 在 AWS Elastic Beanstalk 上托管网站。
B.en: Host the website on AWS Elastic Beanstalk.

C.zh: 使用 Amazon Lightsail 部署网站。
C.en: Deploy the website with Amazon Lightsail.

D.zh: 在 Amazon S3 上托管网站。
D.en: Host the website on Amazon S3.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon EC2 是一种虚拟服务器解决方案，适用于需要自定义服务器配置的动态网站或应用程序。在EC2上部署静态网站需要手动管理服务器、操作系统更新和安全性补丁，因此运维开销较高。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon EC2 is a virtual server solution suitable for dynamic websites or applications that require custom server configurations. Deployment of static websites on EC2 requires manual management of servers, operating system updates and security patches, which results in high operation and maintenance overhead.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Elastic Beanstalk 是一种平台即服务 (PaaS) 解决方案，适用于动态网站或复杂应用程序。虽然它简化了应用程序部署，但使用 Elastic Beanstalk 过于复杂，对于静态网站来说是不必要的。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Elastic Beanstalk is a Platform as a Service (PaaS) solution suitable for dynamic websites or complex applications. While it simplifies application deployment, using Elastic Beanstalk is overly complex and unnecessary for static websites.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon Lightsail 是一种简化的虚拟服务器解决方案，适用于小型应用程序或动态网站。虽然比EC2更容易使用，但仍需要管理服务器，而且运维开销比Amazon S3高。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon Lightsail is a simplified virtual server solution suitable for small applications or dynamic websites. Although it is easier to use than EC2, it still requires management servers, and the operation and maintenance overhead is higher than Amazon S3.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因：Amazon S3 是支持静态网站托管功能的对象存储服务。无需服务器管理，运维开销极小，性价比极高，非常适合静态网站托管。
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: Amazon S3 is an object storage service that supports static website hosting functions. It requires no server management, has minimal operation and maintenance overhead, and is highly cost-effective, making it ideal for static website hosting.

answer_text.zh: D. 在 Amazon S3 上托管网站。
answer_text.en: D. Host the website on Amazon S3.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：D (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：Amazon S3 是支持静态网站托管功能的对象存储服务。无需服务器管理，运维开销极小，性价比极高，非常适合静态网站托管。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Amazon S3 is an object storage service that supports static website hosting functions. It requires no server management, has minimal operation and maintenance overhead, and is highly cost-effective, making it ideal for static website hosting.

questions.domain: 領域 3：雲端技術與服務 (Cloud Technology and Services)

question_text.zh: 一家公司希望在单一 AWS 区域的多个可用区中部署应用程序。此次部署将为公司带来哪些好处？
question_text.en: A company wants to deploy an application in multiple Availability Zones in a single AWS Region. Which benefit will this deployment provide to the company?

A.zh: 为全球客户改进了连接性能
A.en: Improved connection performance for global customers

B.zh: 弹性架构和高度可用的解决方案
B.en: Resilient architecture and a highly available solution

C.zh: 降低整体数据存储成本
C.en: Reduced overall data storage costs

D.zh: 能够在需求低迷期间关闭可用区
D.en: Ability to shut down an Availability Zone during periods of low demand

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：部署多个可用区主要是为了提高应用程序的高可用和容错能力，而不是直接提高全球客户的连接性能。提高全球连接性能通常需要使用 Amazon CloudFront 或 Global Accelerators 等服务。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The deployment of multiple availability zones is mainly to improve the high availability and fault tolerance of applications, but not to directly improve the connection performance of global customers. Improving global connectivity performance often requires using services such as Amazon CloudFront or Global Accelerators.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：将应用程序放置在多个可用区中，可以确保即使一个可用区发生故障，应用程序仍然可以运行，从而实现弹性架构和高可用性解决方案。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: Placing applications in multiple Availability Zones ensures that even if one Availability Zone fails, the application can still run, thereby achieving a resilient architecture and high availability solution.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：部署多个可用区并不会直接降低数据存储成本。数据存储成本通常与 Amazon S3 或 Amazon EBS 等存储服务的使用和定价相关。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Deployment of multiple Availability Zones does not directly reduce data storage costs. Data storage costs are typically tied to usage and pricing of storage services such as Amazon S3 or Amazon EBS.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS 可用区由 AWS 管理，用户无法主动关闭可用区。低需求时期的成本优化通常是通过调整资源大小（例如使用 Auto Scaling）来实现的。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: AWS availability zones are managed by AWS, and users cannot actively close an availability zone. Cost optimization during periods of low demand is often achieved by adjusting the size of resources (such as using Auto Scaling).

answer_text.zh: B. 弹性架构和高度可用的解决方案
answer_text.en: B. Resilient architecture and a highly available solution

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：将应用程序放置在多个可用区中，可以确保即使一个可用区发生故障，应用程序仍然可以运行，从而实现弹性架构和高可用性解决方案。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Placing applications in multiple Availability Zones ensures that even if one Availability Zone fails, the application can still run, thereby achieving a resilient architecture and high availability solution.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Enterprise Support也提供了API交互能力，但其成本比Business Support高很多，所以不是最划算的选择。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Enterprise Support also provides API interaction capabilities, but its cost is much higher than Business Support, so it is not the most cost-effective option.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (50%) B (46%) 4%。热门讨论与考点摘要：商业支持计划允许用户通过 API 与 AWS Support 交互，同时提供更广泛的支持功能，并且比企业支持更具成本效益。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (50%) B (46%) 4%. Popular discussion and key point summary: The Business Support plan allows users to interact with AWS Support through APIs while providing a wider range of support capabilities and is more cost-effective than Enterprise Support.

questions.domain: 領域 1：雲端概念 (Cloud Concepts)

question_text.zh: 一家公司迁移到 AWS 云。现在，该公司根据需要支付服务费用。公司受益于云计算的哪些优势？
question_text.en: A company migrated to the AWS Cloud. Now the company pays for services on an as-needed basis. Which advantage of cloud computing is the company benefiting from?

A.zh: 停止花钱运行和维护数据中心
A.en: Stop spending money running and maintaining data centers

B.zh: 提高速度与敏捷性
B.en: Increase speed and agility

C.zh: 几分钟内走向全球
C.en: Go global in minutes

D.zh: 将固定费用换成可变费用
D.en: Trade fixed expense for variable expense

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：虽然迁移到云可以降低运行和维护数据中心的成本，但这不是这个问题的核心焦点。这个问题强调的是按需付费模式，而不是数据中心的维护。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: While moving to the cloud can reduce the cost of running and maintaining a data center, this is not the core focus of this question. This question emphasizes the pay-as-you-go model rather than the maintenance of the data center.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：速度和敏捷性的提高是云计算的另一个优势，但它与这个问题的「按需付费」描述没有直接关系。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Increased speed and agility is another advantage of cloud computing, but it is not directly related to the "pay-as-you-go" description of this question.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：实现全球化是云计算的优势之一，但与本问题的「按需付费」描述没有直接关系。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Achieving globalization is one of the advantages of cloud computing, but it is not directly related to the "pay-as-you-go" description of this question.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因：这个选项直接描述了按量付费的核心好处，即将传统的固定费用（例如购买硬件）转换为基于使用情况的可变费用。
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: This option directly describes the core benefit of pay-as-you-go, which is converting traditional fixed charges (such as purchasing hardware) into variable charges based on usage.

answer_text.zh: D. 将固定费用换成可变费用
answer_text.en: D. Trade fixed expense for variable expense

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：D (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：这个选项直接描述了按量付费的核心好处，即将传统的固定费用（例如购买硬件）转换为基于使用情况的可变费用。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This option directly describes the core benefit of pay-as-you-go, which is converting traditional fixed charges (such as purchasing hardware) into variable charges based on usage.

questions.domain: 領域 4：計費、定價與支援 (Billing, Pricing, and Support)

question_text.zh: 一家公司将在未来 3 年内在 Amazon EC2 实例上运行可预测的计算工作负载。工作量对公司来说至关重要。该公司希望优化运行工作负载的成本。哪种解决方案可以满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company will run a predictable compute workload on Amazon EC2 instances for the next 3 years. The workload is critical for the company. The company wants to optimize costs to run the workload. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 竞价型实例
A.en: Spot Instances

B.zh: 专用主机
B.en: Dedicated Hosts

C.zh: 节省计划
C.en: Savings Plans

D.zh: 按需型实例
D.en: On-Demand Instances

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：竞价型实例 是 AWS 提供的按需计算资源。价格低，但随时可能中断。由于问题提到工作负载是「关键」的，竞价型实例不适合这种场景，因此不符合题目要求。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: are on-demand computing resources provided by AWS. The price is low, but they may be interrupted at any time. Since the question mentions that the workload is "critical", Spot instances are not suitable for this scenario, so this option is wrong.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：专用主机 提供物理服务器的独占使用权，适用于满足合规或授权要求的场景。虽然它可以提供稳定性，但它的成本更高，并且不是成本优化的最佳选择，所以这个选择是错误的。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: provide exclusive use rights to physical servers and are suitable for scenarios that meet compliance or licensing requirements. Although it can provide stability, it is more expensive and is not the best choice for cost optimization, so this option is wrong.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：节省计划 是一种灵活的定价模型，允许用户承诺一定数量的计算资源以换取折扣。对于长期的、可预测的工作负载，这是优化成本的最佳选择，因此符合题目要求。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: are a flexible pricing model that allows users to commit to a certain amount of computing resources in exchange for a discount. For long-term, predictable workloads, this is the best option to optimize cost, so this option is.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：按需型实例 提供灵活性，但价格更昂贵，并且不适合长时间运行的可预测工作负载，因此不符合题目要求。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: provide flexibility, but are more expensive and are not suitable for long-running predictable workloads, so this option is.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：节省计划 是一种灵活的定价模型，允许用户承诺一定数量的计算资源以换取折扣。对于长期的、可预测的工作负载，这是优化成本的最佳选择，因此符合题目要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: are a flexible pricing model that allows users to commit to a certain amount of computing resources in exchange for a discount. For long-term, predictable workloads, this is the best option to optimize cost, so this option is.

questions.domain: 領域 4：計費、定價與支援 (Billing, Pricing, and Support)

question_text.zh: 一家公司希望在迁移之前估算其 AWS 架构解决方案的成本。哪些 AWS 服务或功能可以满足此要求？
question_text.en: A company wants to estimate the cost for its AWS architecture solution before migration. Which AWS service or feature will meet this requirement?

A.zh: Amazon Detective
A.en: Amazon Detective

B.zh: AWS Budgets
B.en: AWS Budgets

C.zh: AWS Resource Explorer
C.en: AWS Resource Explorer

D.zh: AWS Pricing Calculator
D.en: AWS Pricing Calculator

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon Detective 是一项安全服务，可协助分析和识别潜在的安全问题。它与成本估算无关，因此不符合题目要求。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: is a security service that helps analyze and identify potential security issues. It has nothing to do with cost estimation, so this option is wrong.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Budgets 是用于设置和管理成本和使用预算的工具。虽然它可以帮助监控成本，但它主要用于预算管理，而不是用于迁移前成本估算，因此不符合题目要求。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: is a tool for setting and managing cost and usage budgets. Although it can help monitor costs, it is primarily used for budget management and not for pre-migration cost estimation, so this option is.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Resource Explorer 是一个用于搜索和发现 AWS 资源的工具。它与成本估算无关，所以该选项不符合题目要求。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: is a tool for searching and discovering AWS resources. It has nothing to do with cost estimates, so this option is.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS Pricing Calculator 是用来估算 AWS 服务成本的工具。它非常适合在迁移之前进行成本估算，因此此选项就是如此。
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: The AWS Pricing Calculator is a tool for estimating the cost of AWS services. It is ideal for cost estimation before migration, so this option is.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：D (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS Pricing Calculator 是用来估算 AWS 服务成本的工具。它非常适合在迁移之前进行成本估算，因此此选项就是如此。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: The AWS Pricing Calculator is a tool for estimating the cost of AWS services. It is ideal for cost estimation before migration, so this option is.

questions.domain: 領域 3：雲端技術與服務 (Cloud Technology and Services)

question_text.zh: 一家公司希望集中管理其员工对多个 AWS 账户的访问。公司应使用哪种 AWS 服务或功能来满足此要求？
question_text.en: A company wants to centrally manage its employee's access to multiple AWS accounts. Which AWS service or feature should the company use to meet this requirement?

A.zh: AWS Identity and Access Management 访问分析器
A.en: AWS Identity and Access Management Access Analyzer

B.zh: AWS Secrets Manager
B.en: AWS Secrets Manager

C.zh: AWS IAM Identity Center
C.en: AWS IAM Identity Center

D.zh: AWS Security Token Service (AWS STS)
D.en: AWS Security Token Service (AWS STS)

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：访问分析器是用于分析和验证 IAM 策略并协助识别外部对资源的访问权限的工具，但它不用于集中管理用户对多个账户的访问。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Access Analyzer is a tool used to analyze and verify IAM policies and help identify external access permissions to resources, but it is not used to centrally manage user access to multiple accounts.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Secrets Manager 是一项用于安全存储和管理敏感信息（例如数据库密码、API 密钥等）的服务，与用户访问管理无关。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Secrets Manager is a service used to securely store and manage sensitive information (such as database passwords, API keys, etc.) and has nothing to do with user access management.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：IAM Identity Center（以前称为 AWS Single Sign-On）是一项专门设计用于集中管理用户对多个 AWS 账户和应用程序的访问的服务。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: IAM Identity Center (formerly AWS Single Sign-On) is a service specifically designed to centrally manage user access to multiple AWS accounts and applications.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：STS用于暂时产生访问权限的安全性令牌，但不提供集中管理用户访问的功能。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: STS is used to temporarily generate security tokens for access rights, but it does not provide the functionality to centrally manage user access.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：IAM Identity Center（以前称为 AWS Single Sign-On）是一项专门设计用于集中管理用户对多个 AWS 账户和应用程序的访问的服务。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: IAM Identity Center (formerly AWS Single Sign-On) is a service specifically designed to centrally manage user access to multiple AWS accounts and applications.

questions.domain: 領域 4：計費、定價與支援 (Billing, Pricing, and Support)

question_text.zh: 某大学获得使用 AWS 服务进行研究的资助。研究团队需要确保补助金能持续整个学年。团队已决定每月分配金额总计为总资助金额。如果支出超出计划金额，哪些 AWS 服务或功能会通知团队？
question_text.en: A university receives a grant to conduct research by using AWS services. The research team needs to make sure the grant money lasts for the entire school year. The team has decided on a monthly allocation that adds up to the total grant amount. Which AWS service or feature will notify the team if spending exceeds the planned amount?

A.zh: AWS Budgets
A.en: AWS Budgets

B.zh: 成本管理器
B.en: Cost Explorer

C.zh: 成本分配标签
C.en: Cost allocation tags

D.zh: 成本类别
D.en: Cost categories

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS Budgets 允许用户设置预算并在超出预算时发送通知。这正是问题中所需要的功能。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: AWS Budgets allows users to set budgets and send notifications when budgets are exceeded. This is exactly the function required in the question.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Cost Explorer 主要用于分析和可视化成本数据，但不提供预算通知功能。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: Cost Explorer is primarily used to analyze and visualize cost data, but it does not provide budget notification functionality.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：成本分配标签用于对成本进行标记和分类，但它们不会直接通知用户预算超支。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: Cost allocation tags are used to label and categorize costs, but they do not directly notify users of budget overruns.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：成本类别用于对成本进行组织和分类，但不具有预算通知功能。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: Cost categories are used to organize and classify costs, but they do not have budget notification capabilities.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：A (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS Budgets 允许用户设置预算并在超出预算时发送通知。这正是问题中所需要的功能。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This option is described as follows: AWS Budgets allows users to set budgets and send notifications when budgets are exceeded. This is exactly the function required in the question.

questions.domain: 領域 4：計費、定價與支援 (Billing, Pricing, and Support)

question_text.zh: 一家公司已将工作负载迁移到 AWS 云。该公司希望优化现有的 Amazon EC2 资源。哪些 AWS 服务或工具提供此功能？ （选两个。）
question_text.en: A company has migrated its workload to the AWS Cloud. The company wants to optimize existing Amazon EC2 resources. Which AWS services or tools provide this functionality? (Choose two.)

A.zh: AWS Elastic Beanstalk
A.en: AWS Elastic Beanstalk

B.zh: AWS Cost Explorer
B.en: AWS Cost Explorer

C.zh: Amazon Detective
C.en: Amazon Detective

D.zh: AWS Compute Optimizer
D.en: AWS Compute Optimizer

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Elastic Beanstalk 用于部署和管理应用程序，不会分析现有 EC2 资源并提供成本或实例大小优化建议。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: AWS Elastic Beanstalk is used to deploy and manage applications and does not analyze existing EC2 resources and provide cost or instance size optimization recommendations.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS Cost Explorer可以帮助用户分析和优化成本，包括EC2实例的使用情况，间接支持资源优化。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: AWS Cost Explorer can help users analyze and optimize costs, including EC2 instance usage, thereby indirectly supporting resource optimization.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon Detective 是一项安全分析服务，用于调查潜在的安全问题，与优化 EC2 资源无关。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: Amazon Detective is a security analysis service used to investigate potential security issues and has nothing to do with optimizing EC2 resources.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS Compute Optimizer 专门用于分析和优化 EC2 实例的配置和使用情况，是直接相关的服务。
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: AWS Compute Optimizer is specifically used to analyze and optimize the configuration and usage of EC2 instances and is a directly related service.

E.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Billing Conductor 是一个用于组织和分配账单的计费管理工具，与优化 EC2 资源无关。
E.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: AWS Billing Conductor is a billing management tool for organizing and allocating bills and has nothing to do with optimizing EC2 resources.

answer_text.zh: B. AWS Cost Explorer；D. AWS Compute Optimizer
answer_text.en: B. AWS Cost Explorer; D. AWS Compute Optimizer

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：BD (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS Cost Explorer可以帮助用户分析和优化成本，包括EC2实例的使用情况，间接支持资源优化。；AWS Compute Optimizer 专门用于分析和优化 EC2 实例的配置和使用情况，是直接相关的服务。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: BD (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This option is described as follows: AWS Cost Explorer can help users analyze and optimize costs, including EC2 instance usage, thereby indirectly supporting resource optimization.; This option is described as follows: AWS Compute Optimizer is specifically used to analyze and optimize the configuration and usage of EC2 instances and is a directly related service.

questions.domain: 領域 3：雲端技術與服務 (Cloud Technology and Services)

question_text.zh: 一家拥有多个账户和团队的公司想要创建一个新的多账户 AWS 环境。哪种 AWS 服务支持此要求？
question_text.en: A company with multiple accounts and teams wants to set up a new multi-account AWS environment. Which AWS service supports this requirement?

A.zh: AWS CloudFormation
A.en: AWS CloudFormation

B.zh: AWS Control Tower
B.en: AWS Control Tower

C.zh: AWS Config
C.en: AWS Config

D.zh: Amazon 虚拟私有云 (Amazon VPC)
D.en: Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC)

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS CloudFormation 主要用于基础设施即代码（IaC），帮助用户定义和部署AWS资源模板。虽然它可以跨账户部署资源，但它并不直接支持多账户环境的设置和管理，所以该选项不符合题目要求。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: is mainly used for Infrastructure as Code (IaC) to help users define and deploy AWS resource templates. Although it can deploy resources across accounts, it does not directly support the setup and management of multi-account environments, so this option is.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS Control Tower 是一项专门为设置和管理多账户 AWS 环境而设计的服务。它提供了账户创建、治理、最佳实践和安全管理功能，非常适合本题的要求。因此该选项符合题目要求。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: is a service specifically designed for setting up and managing multi-account AWS environments. It provides account creation, governance, best practices, and security management functions, which is very suitable for the requirements of this question. Therefore this option is.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Config 主要用于资源配置的审计和合规性检查，帮助用户追踪资源配置的变更。它不负责设置多账户环境，因此不符合题目要求。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: is mainly used for auditing and compliance checking of resource configuration, helping users track resource configuration changes. It is not responsible for setting up a multi-account environment, so this option is.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon VPC 用于创建隔离的网络环境。它主要关注网络层面的资源管理，与多账户环境的设置无关，所以该选项不符合题目要求。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon VPC is used to create an isolated network environment. It mainly focuses on resource management at the network level and has nothing to do with the settings of a multi-account environment, so this option is.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS Control Tower 是一项专门为设置和管理多账户 AWS 环境而设计的服务。它提供了账户创建、治理、最佳实践和安全管理功能，非常适合本题的要求。因此该选项符合题目要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: is a service specifically designed for setting up and managing multi-account AWS environments. It provides account creation, governance, best practices, and security management functions, which is very suitable for the requirements of this question. Therefore this option is.

questions.domain: 領域 4：計費、定價與支援 (Billing, Pricing, and Support)

question_text.zh: 公司需要访问检查和建议，以协助公司遵循 AWS 成本优化、安全性、容错、性能和服务配额最佳实践。 AWS 账户上的 AWS 服务和 AWS Support 计划的哪种组合可以满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company needs access to checks and recommendations that help the company follow AWS best practices for cost optimization, security, fault tolerance, performance, and service quotas. Which combination of an AWS service and AWS Support plan on the AWS account will meet these requirements?

A.zh: AWS Trusted Advisor 和 AWS 开发人员支持
A.en: AWS Trusted Advisor with AWS Developer Support

B.zh: AWS Health Dashboard 与 AWS Enterprise Support
B.en: AWS Health Dashboard with AWS Enterprise Support

C.zh: AWS Trusted Advisor 与 AWS 商业支持
C.en: AWS Trusted Advisor with AWS Business Support

D.zh: AWS Health Dashboard 与 AWS Enterprise On-Ramp 支持
D.en: AWS Health Dashboard with AWS Enterprise On-Ramp Support

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS 开发人员支持计划仅提供有限的支持功能，并且不会解锁 AWS Trusted Advisor 的所有检查和建议（例如成本优化、安全性等）。 Developer Support适合开发环境，但不符合问题中全面检查和建议的需要。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The AWS Developer Support plan only provides limited support capabilities and does not unlock all checks and recommendations of AWS Trusted Advisor (such as cost optimization, security, etc.). Developer Support is suitable for development environments, but does not meet the need for comprehensive inspection and suggestions in the question.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Health Dashboard 主要用于提供账户和服务的运行状况信息，而不是提供全面的优化建议。尽管企业支持计划提供高级支持，但问题要求「检查和建议」，这是 AWS Trusted Advisor 的功能，而不是 AWS Health Dashboard 的功能。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: AWS Health Dashboard is mainly used to provide health information for accounts and services, rather than providing comprehensive optimization recommendations. Although the Enterprise Support plan provides advanced support, the question asks for "inspections and recommendations," which is a function of AWS Trusted Advisor, not AWS Health Dashboard.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS Trusted Advisor 提供全面的检查和建议，包括成本优化、安全性、容错、性能和服务配额。 AWS Business Support 计划解锁了 AWS Trusted Advisor 的所有检查功能并满足问题要求。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: AWS Trusted Advisor provides comprehensive inspections and recommendations, including cost optimization, security, fault tolerance, performance, and service quotas. The AWS Business Support plan unlocks all checking functions of AWS Trusted Advisor and meets the question requirements.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Health Dashboard 的功能与选项 B 类似，主要提供健康信息而非优化建议。虽然 Enterprise On-Ramp Support 计划提供了更高级别的支持，但与 AWS Health Dashboard 结合使用时无法满足问题要求。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The function of AWS Health Dashboard is similar to option B, mainly providing health information rather than optimization suggestions. Although the Enterprise On-Ramp Support plan provides a higher level of support, it cannot meet the question requirements when combined with AWS Health Dashboard.

answer_text.zh: C. AWS Trusted Advisor 与 AWS 商业支持
answer_text.en: C. AWS Trusted Advisor with AWS Business Support

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (86%) 14%。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS Trusted Advisor 提供全面的检查和建议，包括成本优化、安全性、容错、性能和服务配额。 AWS Business Support 计划解锁了 AWS Trusted Advisor 的所有检查功能并满足问题要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (86%) 14%. Popular discussion and key point summary: AWS Trusted Advisor provides comprehensive inspections and recommendations, including cost optimization, security, fault tolerance, performance, and service quotas. The AWS Business Support plan unlocks all checking functions of AWS Trusted Advisor and meets the question requirements.

questions.domain: 領域 3：雲端技術與服務 (Cloud Technology and Services)

question_text.zh: 哪种 AWS 服务可协助用户规划和追踪将服务器和应用程序清单数据迁移到 AWS？
question_text.en: Which AWS service helps users plan and track their server and application inventory migration data to AWS?

A.zh: Amazon CloudWatch
A.en: Amazon CloudWatch

B.zh: AWS DataSync
B.en: AWS DataSync

C.zh: AWS Migration Hub
C.en: AWS Migration Hub

D.zh: AWS Application Migration Service
D.en: AWS Application Migration Service

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon CloudWatch主要用于监控和管理AWS资源和应用程序的性能指标，与迁移数据的规划和追踪无关。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: Amazon CloudWatch is mainly used to monitor and manage the performance indicators of AWS resources and applications, and has nothing to do with the planning and tracking of migration data.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS DataSync 用于自动化数据传输并协助用户将数据从本地存储迁移到 AWS，但它不负责规划和追踪迁移的数据。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: AWS DataSync is used to automate data transfer and help users migrate data from local storage to AWS, but it is not responsible for planning and tracking migrated data.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS Migration Hub 是一项专门为帮助用户规划和追踪服务器和应用程序迁移数据而设计的服务，提供导出式的可视化过渡。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: AWS Migration Hub is a service specifically designed to help users plan and track server and application migration data, providing an export-style visual transition. D(25%).

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Application Migration Service 用于将物理服务器或虚拟机迁移到 AWS，但不提供导入式迁移规划和追踪功能。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: AWS Application Migration Service is used to migrate physical servers or virtual machines to AWS, but it does not provide import-style migration planning and tracking functions.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (75%) D (25%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS Migration Hub 是一项专门为帮助用户规划和追踪服务器和应用程序迁移数据而设计的服务，提供导出式的可视化过渡。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (75%) D (25%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This option is described as follows: AWS Migration Hub is a service specifically designed to help users plan and track server and application migration data, providing an export-style visual transition. D(25%).

questions.domain: 領域 3：雲端技術與服務 (Cloud Technology and Services)

question_text.zh: 哪个 AWS 团队或产品可以帮助用户通过在多个专业实践领域中的任何一个领域的付费参与来加速云采用？
question_text.en: Which AWS team or offering helps users accelerate cloud adoption through paid engagements in any of several specialty practice areas?

A.zh: AWS 企业支持
A.en: AWS Enterprise Support

B.zh: AWS 解决方案架构师
B.en: AWS solutions architects

C.zh: AWS 专业服务
C.en: AWS Professional Services

D.zh: AWS 客户经理
D.en: AWS account managers

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Enterprise Support 主要提供技术支持与指导，协助企业解决技术问题，但并非专为加速云采用而设计的服务。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: AWS Enterprise Support mainly provides technical support and guidance to help enterprises solve technical problems, but it is not a service specifically designed to accelerate cloud adoption.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS 解决方案架构师提供架构设计建议，但他们的角色通常是免费咨询服务，而不是加速云采用的付费参与。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: AWS Solutions Architects provide architectural design advice, but their role is typically that of a free consulting service rather than a paid engagement to accelerate cloud adoption.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS 专业服务是一支付费服务团队，专注于协助客户加速云采用，并提供涵盖多个专业领域的专业指导和实际支持。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: AWS Professional Services is a paid service team that focuses on helping customers accelerate cloud adoption and provides professional guidance and practical support covering multiple professional areas.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS 客户经理的主要职责是管理客户关系和销售支持，而不是直接参与加速云采用的技术实践。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The primary responsibility of AWS account managers is to manage customer relationships and sales support, rather than to be directly involved in technical practices that accelerate cloud adoption.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS 专业服务是一支付费服务团队，专注于协助客户加速云采用，并提供涵盖多个专业领域的专业指导和实际支持。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This option is described as follows: AWS Professional Services is a paid service team that focuses on helping customers accelerate cloud adoption and provides professional guidance and practical support covering multiple professional areas.

questions.domain: 領域 4：計費、定價與支援 (Billing, Pricing, and Support)

question_text.zh: 某公司需要购买 Amazon EC2 实例来支持连续运行 1 年以上的应用程序。哪种 EC2 实例购买选项最经济且高效地满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company needs to purchase Amazon EC2 instances to support an application that will run continuously for more than 1 year. Which EC2 instance purchasing option meets these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

A.zh: 专用实例
A.en: Dedicated Instances

B.zh: 竞价型实例
B.en: Spot Instances

C.zh: 预留实例
C.en: Reserved Instances

D.zh: 按需型实例
D.en: On-Demand Instances

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：专用实例 是在物理隔离的硬件上运行的专用实例，通常用于满足合规性或特殊安全性需求。成本较高，不适合长期应用场景，尤其是对成本敏感的场景。原因 不符合「最具性价比」的要求。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: are dedicated instances that run on physically isolated hardware and are typically used to meet compliance or special security needs. The cost is high and it is not suitable for long-term application scenarios, especially cost-sensitive scenarios. Reason for Does not meet the "most cost-effective" requirement.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：竞价型实例 是价格低廉的按需 竞价型实例，但可能随时中断。适合短期任务或容错性高的应用，但不适合需要连续运行1年以上的应用。原因 不符合「持续经营」的要求。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: are on-demand Spot Instances with low prices, but may be interrupted at any time. Suitable for short-term tasks or applications with high fault tolerance, but not suitable for applications that require continuous operation for more than 1 year. Reason for the The requirement of "continuous operation" is not met.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：理由：符合「连续运转1年以上」、「性价比最高」的要求。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: reasons: Meet the requirements of "continuous operation for more than 1 year" and "most cost-effective".

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：按小时计费，不需要长期承诺，适合短期或不可预测的工作负载。成本较高，不适合长期运行的应用。原因 不符合「最具性价比」的要求。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: are billed by the hour, require no long-term commitment, and are suitable for short-term or unpredictable workloads. The cost is higher and not suitable for long-term running applications. Reason for Does not meet the "most cost-effective" requirement.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：理由：符合「连续运转1年以上」、「性价比最高」的要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: reasons: Meet the requirements of "continuous operation for more than 1 year" and "most cost-effective".

questions.domain: 領域 3：雲端技術與服務 (Cloud Technology and Services)

question_text.zh: AWS Cloud Development Kit (AWS CDK) 目前支持哪些编程语言？ （选两个。）
question_text.en: Which programming languages does AWS Cloud Development Kit (AWS CDK) currently support? (Choose two.)

A.zh: Python
A.en: Python

B.zh: 斯威夫特
B.en: Swift

C.zh: 打字稿
C.en: TypeScript

D.zh: 红宝石
D.en: Ruby

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS CDK 支持 Python 作为编程语言。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: AWS CDK supports Python as a programming language.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Swift 不是 AWS CDK 目前支持的编程语言。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: Swift is not a currently supported programming language by AWS CDK.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：TypeScript 是 AWS CDK 主要支持的语言之一。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: TypeScript is one of the main supported languages ​​of AWS CDK.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Ruby 不是 AWS CDK 目前支持的编程语言。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: Ruby is not a currently supported programming language by AWS CDK.

E.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：PHP 不是 AWS CDK 目前支持的编程语言。
E.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: PHP is not a currently supported programming language by AWS CDK.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：AC (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS CDK 支持 Python 作为编程语言。；TypeScript 是 AWS CDK 主要支持的语言之一。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: AC (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: AWS CDK supports Python as a programming language.; This option is described as follows: TypeScript is one of the main supported languages ​​of AWS CDK.

questions.domain: 領域 3：雲端技術與服務 (Cloud Technology and Services)

question_text.zh: 哪一种 AWS 服务或功能使用户能够以程序设置方式预置 AWS 基础设施？
question_text.en: Which AWS service or feature gives users the ability to provision AWS infrastructure programmatically?

A.zh: AWS Cloud Development Kit (AWS CDK)
A.en: AWS Cloud Development Kit (AWS CDK)

B.zh: Amazon CodeGuru
B.en: Amazon CodeGuru

C.zh: AWS Config
C.en: AWS Config

D.zh: AWS CodeCommit
D.en: AWS CodeCommit

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS CDK 是一个开源软件开发桶框架，允许用户使用熟悉的编程语言（例如 JavaScript、Python 等）定义和部署 AWS 基础设施。它是专门为以编程方式管理 AWS 资源而设计的工具。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: AWS CDK is an open source software development bucket framework that allows users to define and deploy AWS infrastructure using familiar programming languages ​​(such as JavaScript, Python, etc.). It is a tool specifically designed for programmatically managing AWS resources.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon CodeGuru 是一种机器学习驱动的工具，用于代码品质分析和性能优化，与基础设施的程序配置无关。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: Amazon CodeGuru is a machine learning-driven tool for code quality analysis and performance optimization, regardless of the programming configuration of the infrastructure.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Config 是一项记录和审核 AWS 资源配置更改并帮助用户确保资源满足合规性要求的服务，但它不提供配置基础设施的编程方式。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: AWS Config is a service that records and audits configuration changes to AWS resources and helps users ensure that resources meet compliance requirements, but it does not provide a programmatic way to configure infrastructure.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS CodeCommit 是一种托管源代码控制服务，用于存储代码存储库，与基础设施的程序配置无关。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option is described as follows: AWS CodeCommit is a managed source code control service for storing code repositories, independent of the programmatic configuration of infrastructure.

answer_text.zh: A. AWS Cloud Development Kit (AWS CDK)
answer_text.en: A. AWS Cloud Development Kit (AWS CDK)

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：A (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS CDK 是一个开源软件开发桶框架，允许用户使用熟悉的编程语言（例如 JavaScript、Python 等）定义和部署 AWS 基础设施。它是专门为以编程方式管理 AWS 资源而设计的工具。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: AWS CDK is an open source software development bucket framework that allows users to define and deploy AWS infrastructure using familiar programming languages ​​(such as JavaScript, Python, etc.). It is a tool specifically designed for programmatically managing AWS resources.
